column1: Individual reflection: project insights and personal growth
column6: Reflection is specific and personal; identifies a concrete owned area, a specific skill developed (with an example), a genuine challenge and how it was addressed, and something underestimated at the start that the student now understands well; goes meaningfully beyond the team retrospective.
column9: Reflection is present and mostly personal but some sections are vague, restate the team narrative, or lack concrete grounding.
column12: Reflection is generic, primarily restates the team narrative, or is missing key elements (owned area, specific skill, challenge, or growth).

column1: Contribution evidence: concrete artifacts tied to owned area
column6: 2–3 artifacts provided with links or references; each has a 2–3 sentence caption explaining what it is, what problem it solved, and what it demonstrates about the student's skills; artifacts are specific to the student's work and could credibly be discussed in an interview.
column9: At least one artifact is provided with a caption; relevance to the student's owned area is partial or captions lack specificity.
column12: Artifacts are missing, inaccessible, or not meaningfully tied to the student's individual contributions.

column6: Capstone project entry includes project title and dates, a one-sentence project summary (including the project outcome if it is notable), 3–5 impact-oriented bullets using action verbs and quantified outcomes where possible, a concise tech stack tag line, and a link to the project; bullets describe the student's personal contributions and accomplishments; overall resume is consistently formatted and proofread.
column9: Capstone entry is present and includes most required elements; bullets may be duty-focused rather than impact-focused, or some elements (dates, link, tech stack) are missing.
column12: Capstone section is missing from the resume, or the entry is a placeholder with insufficient detail to be useful in a job application.

column1: Career narrative: connection between project and career goals
column6: Thoughtfully connects the capstone experience to the student's career direction; identifies specific skills to build on; acknowledges honestly if the project revealed a gap or changed direction; states one concrete thing to do differently on the next team, grounded in the capstone experience.
column9: Career narrative is present and mostly connected to the project; some parts are vague or aspirational without grounding in specific capstone experiences.
column12: Career narrative is missing or generic ("I want to work in software engineering and this project helped me improve").

column1: Peer resume review: specificity and helpfulness
column6: Feedback addresses all four required areas (capstone entry, resume readability, career narrative, one concrete suggestion); comments are specific enough that the peer could act on them; tone is professional and collegial; the concrete suggestion identifies a clear change and explains how to make it.
column9: Feedback is present and mostly relevant but some areas are skipped, comments are vague, or the concrete suggestion lacks enough detail to be actionable.
column12: Peer review is missing, too brief to evaluate, or consists only of surface-level praise without substantive feedback.

column6: Reflection document and resume bullets are professionally written; claims are precise and verifiable; no typos or grammatical errors; resume bullets use consistent action verb formatting.
column9: Mostly professional; minor typos or formatting inconsistencies; some bullets drift from action-oriented to duty-oriented.
column12: Unprofessional writing; significant typos or errors; resume bullets are passive, duty-focused, or unprofessional.

column6: Screenshots and artifacts include alt text and captions; links use descriptive text; private artifacts include a screenshot or excerpt for grading; resume is ATS-friendly (no tables, text boxes, or multi-column layouts that break parsing).
column9: Some accessibility practices used; minor gaps (missing alt text, vague link text, or resume format may have minor ATS issues).
column12: Missing alt text or captions; evidence not evaluable due to inaccessible links; resume in a format likely to fail ATS parsing.
